architecture_fit.note: No attention/transformer at all; a Python bracket-depth loop with a dummy cuda tensor — does not address the 'can attention implement CYK' question.

baseline_comparison.note: Relies only on the benchmark's built-in uniform baseline (lift +0.117); no self-constructed strawman or ablation.

faithfulness.note: No model, no causal/ablation evidence, and README does not even mention faithfulness or propose a check.

operating_range.note: Span sweep 3-8 is recorded but robustness is only 0.41 and the README never discusses where it degrades.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Hand-set scores (1.5 balance / 0.75 other) are a hand-built rule, but it is a heuristic not an attention circuit and barely beats chance.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is static text/labels with one example string; no chart, heatmap, or interactive view of the result.

visualisation_rationale.note: 'Why this visualisation' describes an imagined chart that does not exist in app.py; no axes/baseline/grain justification.

notes: A hand-coded bracket-depth heuristic that runs in pure Python and only allocates a token cuda tensor to pass the GPU guard; it is neither an attention mechanism nor a delta from base_model.py, so it does not engage the goal's actual question. It produces a small positive lift over uniform (0.117) but with poor span robustness (0.41), and the Gradio Demo tab is static text with no real visualisation backing the README's claims.
